summary: Mock AI summary of meeting notes.
decisions: Schedule follow-up meeting in two weeks
Prepare a single pilot proposal for review
action_items: Identify key staff for pilot participation
risks_or_concerns: Change management effort may be underestimated

lessons_learned.title: Pilot proposals benefit from early staff input
lessons_learned.description: Involving staff in pilot design early improves buy-in and surfaces practical constraints that would otherwise block adoption later.

lessons_learned.title: Staff capacity concerns must be addressed upfront
lessons_learned.description: Any AI initiative must demonstrate it reduces burden, not adds to it. Frame pilots as time-saving tools from the start.

reusable_insights: Organizations that proactively discuss pilots are typically more ready to experiment than those that need to be convinced.
staff_concerns_or_adoption_concerns: Staff may fear AI replaces rather than assists their work — emphasize augmentation, not automation.
Data privacy and patron confidentiality must be addressed before any pilot begins.
Change management effort is often underestimated — budget time for staff transition and training.
human_judgment_points: All pilot-generated content requires human review before external use — never send AI-generated output without verification.
Staff readiness assessment should be conducted by a human program lead, not automated.
AI recommendations are starting points — all relationship decisions require human judgment and context awareness.
knowledge_sharing_opportunities: Share pilot proposal template and lessons learned with other organizations exploring similar AI use cases.
Staff concern patterns observed here may be valuable input for broader adoption readiness research.

playbook_candidates.title: Pilot Proposal Development Process
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When an organization expresses interest in exploring an AI-assisted workflow pilot during a meeting or call.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. Schedule a scoping meeting within one week. 2. Map current workflow with staff input. 3. Draft a pilot proposal with clear success criteria. 4. Review proposal with organization lead. 5. Refine and confirm pilot scope.

recommended_follow_up: Share pilot proposal and schedule review meeting.

follow_up_tasks.title: Prepare pilot proposal document
follow_up_tasks.description: Draft and share a pilot proposal based on the discussion.

follow_up_tasks.title: Prepare AI literacy workshop materials
follow_up_tasks.description: Develop introductory AI literacy materials tailored to the organization's context.

follow_up_tasks.title: Assess staff readiness for AI tools
follow_up_tasks.description: Conduct a staff readiness assessment to identify training needs and concerns.

current_workflow: Staff coordinate volunteer shifts, track community engagement manually, and produce program reports using spreadsheets and shared documents.
workflow_pain_points: No specific pain points documented — further discussion needed to identify workflow friction.
possible_ai_insertion_points: AI-assisted scheduling and engagement pattern analysis
required_human_review: All AI-generated summaries must be reviewed by a staff member before saving to records.
AI-drafted communications must be approved by a human before sending.
Staff capacity decisions must be made by program leads, not automated.
Final decisions on workflow changes require human approval.
missing_knowledge_or_data: Formal documentation of current workflows is not available — would benefit from a workflow mapping session.
Quantitative data on time spent per task is not yet available — time tracking observation could help.
Policy and compliance requirements are not documented in accessible formats.
Staff training needs and digital literacy levels are not fully assessed.

failure_cases_or_exceptions.what_failed: No specific failure cases documented yet
failure_cases_or_exceptions.why: Interaction notes do not describe past failures or exceptions
failure_cases_or_exceptions.missing_context: Failure mode analysis has not been conducted
failure_cases_or_exceptions.human_review_required: Staff should reflect on past challenges to identify recurring failure patterns
failure_cases_or_exceptions.suggested_prevention: Schedule a retrospective session to document past workflow failures and contributing factors

reusable_workflow_insights: Community-facing organizations typically have undocumented workflows that rely heavily on staff relationships — process documentation is a prerequisite for AI adoption.

summary: Mock AI summary of meeting notes.
decisions: Schedule follow-up meeting in two weeks
Prepare a single pilot proposal for review
action_items: Identify key staff for pilot participation
risks_or_concerns: Change management effort may be underestimated

lessons_learned.title: Documentation pain points reveal automation opportunities
lessons_learned.description: Staff who spend significant time on manual documentation are the strongest candidates for AI-assisted workflow pilots.

reusable_insights: Documentation-heavy workflows are ubiquitous in public-serving organizations and represent a low-barrier entry point for AI assistance.
staff_concerns_or_adoption_concerns: Staff may fear AI replaces rather than assists their work — emphasize augmentation, not automation.
Data privacy and patron confidentiality must be addressed before any pilot begins.
Change management effort is often underestimated — budget time for staff transition and training.
human_judgment_points: Staff readiness assessment should be conducted by a human program lead, not automated.
AI-generated documentation summaries must be reviewed and approved by a designated staff member before saving to records.
AI recommendations are starting points — all relationship decisions require human judgment and context awareness.
knowledge_sharing_opportunities: Documentation workflow insights from this engagement could inform a cross-organizational best-practice guide.

playbook_candidates.title: Documentation Workflow Assessment
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When an organization reports spending significant staff time on manual documentation, note-taking, or report generation.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. Conduct a 30-minute workflow mapping session. 2. Identify top 3 documentation pain points. 3. Select one low-risk process for AI assistance. 4. Define human review steps. 5. Run a 2-week pilot with daily check-ins.

playbook_candidates.title: Pilot Outcome Evaluation Framework
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When a pilot is underway and the organization needs to track whether it is meeting its goals.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. Define 2-3 measurable success indicators before pilot launch. 2. Collect baseline data on current process. 3. Track weekly progress against indicators. 4. Conduct a mid-pilot review with staff. 5. Document lessons learned and decide on next phase.

follow_up_tasks.title: Prepare pilot proposal document
follow_up_tasks.description: Draft and share a pilot proposal based on the discussion.

follow_up_tasks.title: Review documentation workflow
follow_up_tasks.description: Assess current documentation practices and identify areas for AI assistance.

follow_up_tasks.title: Schedule follow-up meeting
follow_up_tasks.description: Book a follow-up meeting to continue the discussion.

current_workflow: Staff manually enter data into spreadsheets or forms, produce recurring reports, and store documents in shared folders. There is no automated workflow.
workflow_pain_points: Manual documentation is time-consuming and inconsistent across staff members.
Repetitive data entry tasks consume staff time that could be spent on direct service.
Community engagement tracking is fragmented — no single view of outreach activities.
Policy compliance requirements add overhead to every step of the workflow.
repetitive_tasks_identified: Filling out recurring forms or reports
Data entry from paper or PDF sources into digital systems
information_or_documents_used: Spreadsheets or databases with program data
possible_ai_insertion_points: AI-assisted meeting note summarization and action item extraction
Automated data extraction and report generation from structured inputs
AI-assisted scheduling and engagement pattern analysis
AI-assisted compliance checking against policy documents
required_human_review: All AI-generated summaries must be reviewed by a staff member before saving to records.
AI-drafted communications must be approved by a human before sending.
Policy compliance decisions require human judgment — AI suggestions are advisory only.
Staff capacity decisions must be made by program leads, not automated.
Final decisions on workflow changes require human approval.
missing_knowledge_or_data: Staff training needs and digital literacy levels are not fully assessed.

failure_cases_or_exceptions.what_failed: Key information from meetings is lost or not shared with relevant staff
failure_cases_or_exceptions.why: No standardized note-taking format or centralized documentation repository
failure_cases_or_exceptions.missing_context: Meeting notes are stored in personal files or emailed to select individuals
failure_cases_or_exceptions.human_review_required: Staff need to decide what information is relevant to share and with whom
failure_cases_or_exceptions.suggested_prevention: Adopt a shared note-taking template and centralized documentation practice with distribution list

failure_cases_or_exceptions.what_failed: Manual data entry errors lead to incorrect reports and rework
failure_cases_or_exceptions.why: No validation checks or automated data entry — entirely dependent on human accuracy
failure_cases_or_exceptions.missing_context: No audit trail for data entry — errors are hard to trace
failure_cases_or_exceptions.human_review_required: Data quality checks need human judgment to distinguish genuine errors from edge cases
failure_cases_or_exceptions.suggested_prevention: Introduce input validation, automated data checks, and regular audit reviews

failure_cases_or_exceptions.what_failed: Policy updates are not consistently communicated or applied across the team
failure_cases_or_exceptions.why: Policy changes are shared via email and rely on individual awareness
failure_cases_or_exceptions.missing_context: No centralized policy repository or version control
failure_cases_or_exceptions.human_review_required: Staff need to interpret how policy changes apply to their specific workflows
failure_cases_or_exceptions.suggested_prevention: Maintain a centralized policy repository with change notifications and mandatory review checkpoints

reusable_workflow_insights: Documentation workflows are a universal pain point across organizations and represent the lowest-barrier entry for AI assistance.
Repetitive administrative tasks are often invisible to leadership — surfacing their time cost is a powerful motivator for change.
Policy compliance workflows are high-risk and high-value targets for AI assistance — but require the most rigorous human oversight.
Community-facing organizations typically have undocumented workflows that rely heavily on staff relationships — process documentation is a prerequisite for AI adoption.

lessons_learned.title: AI literacy builds confidence before tool adoption
lessons_learned.description: Introducing AI concepts through workshops before deploying specific tools reduces anxiety and increases voluntary adoption.

lessons_learned.title: Staff capacity concerns must be addressed upfront
lessons_learned.description: Any AI initiative must demonstrate it reduces burden, not adds to it. Frame pilots as time-saving tools from the start.

reusable_insights: AI literacy training is most effective when delivered in the context of the learner's actual daily work, not as abstract concepts.
staff_concerns_or_adoption_concerns: Staff may fear AI replaces rather than assists their work — emphasize augmentation, not automation.
Data privacy and patron confidentiality must be addressed before any pilot begins.
Change management effort is often underestimated — budget time for staff transition and training.
human_judgment_points: Staff readiness assessment should be conducted by a human program lead, not automated.
AI recommendations are starting points — all relationship decisions require human judgment and context awareness.
knowledge_sharing_opportunities: Workshop materials and curriculum could be adapted for other similar organizations.

playbook_candidates.title: AI Literacy Workshop Delivery
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When an organization requests foundational AI literacy training before committing to specific tool pilots.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. Assess current staff AI awareness through a brief survey. 2. Tailor workshop content to the organization's daily workflows. 3. Deliver a 90-minute interactive session with live demonstrations. 4. Collect feedback and identify pilot interest. 5. Schedule follow-up for interested teams.

playbook_candidates.title: Pilot Outcome Evaluation Framework
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When a pilot is underway and the organization needs to track whether it is meeting its goals.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. Define 2-3 measurable success indicators before pilot launch. 2. Collect baseline data on current process. 3. Track weekly progress against indicators. 4. Conduct a mid-pilot review with staff. 5. Document lessons learned and decide on next phase.

recommended_follow_up: Share workshop outline and confirm logistics.

follow_up_tasks.description: Develop introductory AI literacy materials tailored to the organization's context.

follow_up_tasks.description: Conduct a staff readiness assessment to identify training needs and concerns.

current_workflow: Staff use a mix of email, shared documents, and in-person meetings to coordinate programs. Documentation practices are informal and vary by staff member.
workflow_pain_points: No specific pain points documented — further discussion needed to identify workflow friction.
repetitive_tasks_identified: General administrative coordination — further observation needed
possible_ai_insertion_points: General workflow mapping to identify highest-impact AI insertion points
required_human_review: All AI-generated summaries must be reviewed by a staff member before saving to records.
AI-drafted communications must be approved by a human before sending.
Staff capacity decisions must be made by program leads, not automated.
Final decisions on workflow changes require human approval.

failure_cases_or_exceptions.missing_context: Failure mode analysis has not been conducted
failure_cases_or_exceptions.human_review_required: Staff should reflect on past challenges to identify recurring failure patterns
failure_cases_or_exceptions.suggested_prevention: Schedule a retrospective session to document past workflow failures and contributing factors

reusable_workflow_insights: Organizations that invest in staff training are more likely to adopt AI tools successfully, as they already value skill development.
staff_concerns: Staff may fear AI replaces rather than assists their work — emphasize augmentation, not automation.
evaluation_or_recognition_risks: Unclear how AI-assisted work would be evaluated against current performance criteria.
training_or_followup_needs: Organization has expressed interest in AI literacy training — schedule foundational workshop.
Staff digital literacy assessment recommended before deploying any AI tool.

lessons_learned.title: AI literacy builds confidence before tool adoption
lessons_learned.description: Introducing AI concepts through workshops before deploying specific tools reduces anxiety and increases voluntary adoption.

lessons_learned.title: Structured follow-up processes improve relationship continuity
lessons_learned.description: Recording next actions and follow-up dates during meetings prevents relationship drift and ensures consistent outreach.

reusable_insights: Organizations that proactively discuss pilots are typically more ready to experiment than those that need to be convinced.
AI literacy training is most effective when delivered in the context of the learner's actual daily work, not as abstract concepts.
Meeting follow-up consistency is a leading indicator of relationship health — organizations with structured follow-ups progress faster.
staff_concerns_or_adoption_concerns: No specific adoption concerns raised — monitor during next interaction.
human_judgment_points: All pilot-generated content requires human review before external use — never send AI-generated output without verification.

playbook_candidates.title: Pilot Proposal Development Process
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When an organization expresses interest in exploring an AI-assisted workflow pilot during a meeting or call.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. Schedule a scoping meeting within one week. 2. Map current workflow with staff input. 3. Draft a pilot proposal with clear success criteria. 4. Review proposal with organization lead. 5. Refine and confirm pilot scope.

playbook_candidates.title: Structured Follow-up Protocol
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When an organization has multiple interactions but lacks a consistent follow-up process, leading to relationship drift.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. After each interaction, record next action and follow-up date within 24 hours. 2. Set a weekly review of pending follow-ups. 3. Send recap emails within 48 hours of each meeting. 4. Track follow-up completion rate monthly.

playbook_candidates.title: AI Literacy Workshop Delivery
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When an organization requests foundational AI literacy training before committing to specific tool pilots.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. Assess current staff AI awareness through a brief survey. 2. Tailor workshop content to the organization's daily workflows. 3. Deliver a 90-minute interactive session with live demonstrations. 4. Collect feedback and identify pilot interest. 5. Schedule follow-up for interested teams.

recommended_follow_up: Share pilot proposal and schedule review meeting.

failure_cases_or_exceptions.why: No centralized tracking system — relies on individual memory and email inbox
failure_cases_or_exceptions.missing_context: No automated reminders or shared task list
failure_cases_or_exceptions.human_review_required: Follow-up assignments need human judgment on priority and timing
failure_cases_or_exceptions.suggested_prevention: Implement a shared follow-up tracking system with automated reminders and weekly review

playbook_candidates.title: Grant-Funded Initiative Scoping
playbook_candidates.when_to_use: When an organization wants to explore AI adoption through grant-funded projects and needs help structuring the proposal.
playbook_candidates.suggested_process: 1. Identify the grant program requirements and timeline. 2. Map proposed outcomes to the organization's pain points. 3. Draft measurable success indicators. 4. Outline the pilot timeline with human oversight checkpoints. 5. Review with the organization before submission.
